title: Citrus × latifolia
intro: Citrus × latifolia, commonly known as the Tahiti lime, is a hybrid citrus plant that combines the characteristics of the key lime and the Persian lime. The health benefits of the Tahiti lime include its high content of vitamin C, which supports immune function and antioxidant defenses, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, limonoids, and essential oils, such as limonene and beta-pinene, which contribute to its medicinal and culinary value. The most common herbal preparations made with the Tahiti lime include lime juice, tea, and essential oil, which are used to support digestive health, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote respiratory well-being.
taxonomy: Citrus × latifolia is a hybrid of the pomelo (Citrus maxima) and the sweet orange (Citrus sinensis). It is classified as a species of the genus Citrus, in the family Rutaceae. The plant is commonly referred to as the Meyer lemon, although it is not a true lemon. The classification of Citrus × latifolia is further supported by its unique characteristics and genetic makeup. As a hybrid, it does not fit neatly into the traditional classification of citrus species.
uses: Citrus × latifolia, also known as the Meyer lemon, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The peel and juice of the Meyer lemon have been utilized to treat digestive issues and soothe nausea. Its antiseptic properties have also been harnessed to aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. In addition, the oil extracted from the Meyer lemon peel has been used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. Furthermore, the vitamin C content in Meyer lemons has been recognized for its immune-boosting properties.
benefits: Citrus × latifolia, also known as the etrog or yellow citron, has been associated with several health benefits due to its high content of vitamin C and flavonoids. The antioxidants present in Citrus × latifolia may help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. This plant has also been traditionally used to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of nausea and indigestion. The essential oils extracted from Citrus × latifolia have antimicrobial properties, making them effective against various bacteria and fungi. Incorporating Citrus × latifolia into one's diet may contribute to improved immune function and overall well-being.
properties: Citrus × latifolia, commonly known as the Persian lime, possesses several therapeutic properties. Its high concentration of vitamin C and flavonoids has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the fruit's extract has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help in preventing the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. The essential oil extracted from the peel of the Persian lime has been used in aromatherapy to reduce stress and improve mood. Furthermore, the oil's antiseptic properties make it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine for wound healing and skin infections.
constituents: Citrus × latifolia, also known as the Persian lime, contains several active constituents including limonene, which has been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The fruit also contains flavonoids, such as naringenin and naringin, which may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the peel of the Persian lime contains compounds like limonin and furanocoumarins, which have been reported to have antibacterial and anti-cancer properties. The essential oils extracted from the peel of the fruit are known to contain limonene, beta-pinene, and gamma-terpinene. These active constituents may contribute to the fruit's potential health benefits and uses in traditional medicine.
parts: Citrus × latifolia, commonly known as the Persian lime or Tahiti lime, is a fruit that has been used for various medicinal purposes. The essential oil extracted from the peel of Citrus × latifolia has antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin infections. The fruit's juice has also been used to treat digestive issues and provide relief from symptoms of indigestion. Additionally, the leaves and stems of Citrus × latifolia have been used in traditional medicine to treat fever and respiratory infections. The fruit's high content of vitamin C also makes it a useful remedy for scurvy and other vitamin deficiencies.
preparations: Citrus × latifolia is primarily used in herbal preparations due to its high content of limonene and other bioactive compounds. These compounds are known for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial in various health applications. Herbal preparations made from Citrus × latifolia may be used to support digestive health, reduce stress, and promote immune function. Additionally, extracts from this citrus species are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. The essential oil obtained from Citrus × latifolia is also used in aromatherapy due to its uplifting and refreshing properties.
side_effects: Citrus × latifolia, also known as the Tahiti lime, can cause gastrointestinal issues in some individuals due to its high acidity. Consuming large amounts of Tahiti lime juice can lead to an imbalance of electrolytes, resulting in symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and stomach pain. In rare cases, excessive consumption of Tahiti lime juice may also cause kidney damage due to its high oxalate content. Additionally, people with citrus allergies or sensitivities may experience skin irritation, itching, or hives after coming into contact with the fruit or its juice. It is essential to consume Tahiti lime in moderation and be aware of any adverse reactions.
